questions.subject: History
questions.prompt: Regarding the classification of the National Sports Awards in India, which of the following is NOT considered one of the six core categories of the awards?
questions.explanation: According to the source, there are six primary categories of National Sports Awards: Major Dhyan Chand Khel Ratna, Arjuna Award, Dronacharya Award, Major Dhyan Chand Award, MAKA Trophy, and Rashtriya Khel Protsahan Puraskar. The Tenzing Norgay National Adventure Award has been given out 'along with' the six awards since 2004, but it is not listed as one of the six core categories.
questions.takeaway: The Tenzing Norgay National Adventure Award is associated with the National Sports Awards ceremony but is technically distinct from the six core categories.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: The six categories of the National Sports Awards are: 1. Major Dhyan Chand Khel Ratna Award, 2. Arjuna Award, 3. Dronacharya Award, 4. Major Dhyan Chand Award, 5. Maulana Abul Kalam Azad rolling Trophy... 6. Rashtriya Khel Protsahan Puraskar. *Since 2004, the Tenzing Norgay National Adventure Award has also been given out along with the six National Sports Awards.

questions.subject: History
questions.prompt: Regarding the administration and categories of the National Sports Awards, which of the following statements is correct?

options.text: The Maulana Abul Kalam Azad (MAKA) Trophy is a permanent trophy awarded to individual athletes for academic excellence.
The Tenzing Norgay National Adventure Award is officially classified as one of the six core categories of the National Sports Awards.
The National Sports Day is celebrated annually to mark the day India won its first Olympic gold medal in hockey.
The Rashtriya Khel Protsahan Puraskar is one of the six primary categories of the National Sports Awards.

questions.explanation: Option D is correct as Rashtriya Khel Protsahan Puraskar is listed as one of the six core categories. Option A is incorrect because the MAKA Trophy is a 'rolling' trophy. Option B is incorrect because the Tenzing Norgay award is given 'along with' the six core awards but is not listed as one of the six itself. Option C is incorrect because National Sports Day commemorates Major Dhyan Chand's birth anniversary, not a specific medal date.
questions.takeaway: Distinguish between the six core categories of National Sports Awards and supplementary awards like the Tenzing Norgay National Adventure Award.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: The six categories of the National Sports Awards are: 1. Major Dhyan Chand Khel Ratna Award, 2. Arjuna Award, 3. Dronacharya Award, 4. Major Dhyan Chand Award, 5. Maulana Abul Kalam Azad rolling Trophy... and 6. Rashtriya Khel Protsahan Puraskar. *Since 2004, the Tenzing Norgay National Adventure Award has also been given out along with the six National Sports Awards.

questions.subject: History
questions.prompt: Major Dhyan Chand, in whose honour the National Sports Day is celebrated, played a pivotal role in India winning consecutive Olympic gold medals in which of the following years?
questions.explanation: Major Dhyan Chand was instrumental in India's three consecutive Olympic gold hauls in 1928 (Amsterdam), 1932 (Los Angeles), and 1936 (Berlin).
questions.takeaway: Major Dhyan Chand's Olympic legacy is defined by the pre-World War II hat-trick of gold medals (1928-1936).
questions.evidenceExcerpt: He played a key role in India’s three consecutive gold hauls at the Olympic Games - in 1928, 1932 and 1936.
